title: Add stint-dynamics features from lagged and rolling degradation slopes
summary: The current best runs use static ratios, but pit calls depend on acceleration of degradation, not just level.
rationale: The current best runs use static ratios, but pit calls depend on acceleration of degradation, not just level. Recent F1 tire-degradation work models latent pace decay with state resets at pit stops, implying slope and curvature within stint should matter.
implementation_hint: For each driver-race-stint, derive lag-1/lag-2 changes and rolling regressions for LapTime, LapTime_Delta, Cumulative_Degradation, TyreLife, and position. Add slope, curvature, residual-to-trend, and post-pit reset indicators; compute them strictly from prior laps only.
expected_effect: Should separate genuine cliff-edge stints from long but stable stints, improving recall near true stop windows without relying on leakage-prone target encoding.
risk: Noise from safety-car/wet anomalies can corrupt slopes; robust clipping or race-phase normalization is needed.
